data_out.description: HTTP connectivity check report documenting response status, headers, and timestamp

domain_knowledge: HTTP status codes 2xx indicate successful server response; 4xx and 5xx indicate client and server errors respectively.
Web service availability checks should record response latency and timestamp to establish baseline performance and detect intermittent failures.
Operational deployment requires not only network reachability but also valid HTTP response headers and content-type matching expected service interface.

direct_checks: verify HTTP GET request to https://metabologenomic.cbd.cs.cmu.edu/ returns status code 200 or other 2xx success code
verify response contains valid HTML or JSON (file_format_is text/html or application/json)
verify response body is non-empty (contains_substring of service identifier or title)
verify DNS resolution succeeds for metabologenomic.cbd.cs.cmu.edu
verify TLS/SSL certificate is valid and not expired
expert_review: confirm that the returned page or API response is consistent with a deployed molDiscovery academic web service as described in the repository
assess whether the service interface matches the deployment documentation referenced in the GitHub repository README

workflow_description: 1. Send HTTP GET request to https://metabologenomic.cbd.cs.cmu.edu/ using curl or standard HTTP client. 2. Capture HTTP response status code and response headers. 3. Verify HTTP status code is 200 or other success code (2xx range). 4. Log response metadata (timestamp, latency, content-type) and save to deployment-check report.
